# go

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Generated Go SDK for the OpenMeter v1 REST API. client.gen.go is fully generated by oapi-codegen from api/openapi.cloud.yaml; client.go adds ergonomic wrappers and auth helpers on top of the generated client.

## Patterns

**Never edit client.gen.go** — client.gen.go carries a 'Code generated by oapi-codegen DO NOT EDIT' header and is regenerated by 'go generate' (//go:generate go tool github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen/v2/cmd/oapi-codegen --config=codegen.yaml ../../openapi.cloud.yaml in client.go). Any manual edit is overwritten on next gen-api run. (`// BAD: editing client.gen.go directly
// GOOD: add wrapper to client.go`)
**Ergonomic wrappers live in client.go only** — client.go is the only hand-written Go file. It extends *Client and *ClientWithResponses with convenience methods (NewAuthClientWithResponses, IngestEvent, IngestEventBatch, IngestEventWithResponse, IngestEventBatchWithResponse) that delegate to the verbose generated method names. (`func (c *ClientWithResponses) IngestEventWithResponse(ctx context.Context, event Event, reqEditors ...RequestEditorFn) (*IngestEventsResponse, error) {
	return c.IngestEventsWithApplicationCloudeventsPlusJSONBodyWithResponse(ctx, event, reqEditors...)
}`)
**Auth injected via RequestEditorFn** — NewAuthClientWithResponses and NewAuthClient prepend a WithRequestEditorFn option that sets the Authorization: Bearer header, then append any caller-supplied opts. Never embed auth logic inside generated code. (`func NewAuthClientWithResponses(server, apiSecret string, opts ...ClientOption) (*ClientWithResponses, error) {
	o := []ClientOption{WithRequestEditorFn(func(ctx context.Context, req *http.Request) error {
		req.Header.Set("Authorization", fmt.Sprintf("Bearer %s", apiSecret))
		return nil
	})}
	o = append(opts, o...)
	return NewClientWithResponses(server, o...)
}`)
**Tests use httptest.NewServer mock server** — client_test.go validates each wrapper by spinning up an httptest.Server, asserting on method, path, and Content-Type, and comparing JSON-decoded response structs with assert.Equal from testify. (`server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	assert.Equal(t, http.MethodPost, r.Method)
	assert.Equal(t, "/api/v1/events", r.URL.Path)
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}))
defer server.Close()`)
**Enum types carry a Valid() method** — Generated enums (e.g. AddonStatus, AppType) have a Valid() bool switch that covers all known constants and returns false for unknown values. Use Valid() for runtime validation instead of manual switch statements. (`func (e AppType) Valid() bool {
	switch e {
	case AppTypeCustomInvoicing, AppTypeSandbox, AppTypeStripe:
		return true
	default:
		return false
	}
}`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `client.gen.go` | Fully generated SDK: all types, enums, request/response structs, and HTTP methods. Source of truth is api/openapi.cloud.yaml via codegen.yaml. | Do not edit. Regenerate with 'make gen-api'. Generated with always-prefix-enum-values and disable-required-readonly-as-pointer compatibility flags. |
| `client.go` | Hand-written ergonomic layer: auth constructors and CloudEvents ingest shorthands. The //go:generate directive here drives regeneration of client.gen.go. | Only add methods that delegate to generated methods. Do not duplicate type definitions or business logic here. |
| `codegen.yaml` | oapi-codegen configuration: package name 'openmeter', generates client + models + embedded-spec, output to client.gen.go, with always-prefix-enum-values enabled. | Changing compatibility flags here affects all generated enum constant names and pointer semantics across the SDK. Coordinate with api/openapi.cloud.yaml changes. |
| `client_test.go` | Unit tests for hand-written wrappers using mock HTTP servers. Tests are in package openmeter (white-box). | TetsListEvents has a typo in the function name (missing 't') — it is never run by 'go test'. Do not replicate this pattern. |
| `error.go` | ErrResponse struct for rendering HTTP error responses. Minimal — StatusCode, StatusText, AppCode, Message fields. | This is separate from the generated error types in client.gen.go. Ensure new error handling uses the correct type for the context. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Editing client.gen.go manually — it is overwritten on every 'make gen-api' run
- Adding new API types or request/response structs to client.go — they belong in api/spec/ TypeSpec and regenerated into client.gen.go
- Embedding authentication tokens or credentials directly in client methods instead of using the ClientOption / RequestEditorFn pattern
- Importing app-internal packages (beyond openmeter/pkg/models which client.gen.go already imports) — this is a public SDK and must remain externally importable without the full monorepo
- Bypassing the generated *ClientWithResponses wrapper and writing raw HTTP calls in tests

## Decisions

- **client.gen.go is generated from api/openapi.cloud.yaml (not openapi.yaml) using codegen.yaml with always-prefix-enum-values** — The cloud variant includes cloud-specific endpoints. Enum prefixing prevents collisions when multiple enums share value strings (e.g. 'active' appears in AddonStatus, AppStatus, etc.).
- **Ergonomic wrappers in client.go rather than changing codegen.yaml templates** — CloudEvents ingest has two content-types (single vs batch) that produce verbose generated method names; short wrappers (IngestEventWithResponse, IngestEventBatchWithResponse) improve DX without forking the generator.

## Example: Add a new ergonomic wrapper for a generated method

```
// In client.go — wrap the verbose generated method name:
import (
	"context"
	"net/http"
)

// DeleteCustomerWithResponse wraps the generated verbose name for clarity.
func (c *ClientWithResponses) DeleteCustomerWithResponse(ctx context.Context, customerID string, reqEditors ...RequestEditorFn) (*DeleteCustomerResponse, error) {
	return c.DeleteCustomerByIdWithResponse(ctx, customerID, reqEditors...)
}
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
